pengcy/linkedin-auth2-firebase: Firebase custom auth with LinkedIn Oauth2","url":"https://github.com/pengcy/linkedin-auth2-firebase"},{"title":"Firebase Authentication setup - Angular: Cloud-Powered Apps with Firebase Video Tutorial | LinkedIn Learning, formerly Lynda.com","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/learning/angular-cloud-powered-apps-with-firebase/firebase-authentication-setup"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Firebase Authentication with LinkedIn OAuth provider setup:

## Key Findings:

**Native Support Status:**
Firebase does not natively support LinkedIn as an identity provider.

**Implementation Approaches:**

1. **Custom Authentication Method**: To bridge this gap, you can use Firebase Custom Authentication, which allows you to integrate LinkedIn's OAuth 2.0 flow and securely authenticate users with Firebase.

2. **OIDC Integration**: LinkedIn transitioned from its traditional OAuth API to a more robust OpenID Connect (OIDC) based authentication. However, LinkedIn OIDC hosts the config file at %issuer%/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration which seems to make authentication with LinkedIn incompatible with Firebase Auth and GCP Identity Platform.

3. **Firebase Functions Approach**: Several implementations use Firebase Cloud Functions with Node.js to handle the LinkedIn OAuth flow and create custom Firebase tokens.

**Setup Requirements:**
- A LinkedIn Developer Account and a Firebase Account
- You will need to specify the ID of the identity provider and your client ID and client secret, which you typically get from the provider's developer site

**Current Workarounds:**
The most common approach involves using Firebase Custom Tokens combined with LinkedIn's OIDC flow through a backend service (Firebase Functions or another backend), rather than using Firebase's native OAuth provider integration.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

48	    app.on('second-instance', (event, commandLine) => {
49	      // Someone tried to run a second instance, focus our window
50	      if (win) {

70	  if (!sid) {

72	    return

74	
75	  // Find the matching session

77	  if (!session) {
78	    console.error('No matching auth session found for sid:', sid)
79	    return
80	  }
81	
82	  // Resolve the promise with the sid
83	  session.resolver(sid)
84	  authSessions.delete(sid)

103	})
104

122	  console.log(`Download progress: ${progressObj.percent.toFixed(2)}%`)
123	  win?.webContents.send('update:download-progress', progressObj)
124	})
125	
126	autoUpdater.on('update-downloaded', (info) => {
127	  console.log('Update downloaded:', info.version)

129	})

131	async function createWindow() {
132	  win = new BrowserWindow({
133	    width: 1200,
134	    height: 800,
135	    title: 'Divergent Todos',
136	    webPreferences: {
137	      preload: path.join(__dirname, 'preload.js'),
138	      nodeIntegration: false,
139	      contextIsolation: true,
140	      sandbox: true,
141	    },
142	  })
143	
144	  // Intercept window.open and new window requests
145	  win.webContents.setWindowOpenHandler(({ url }) => {
146	    // Allow navigation to app's own domain/localhost in dev
147	    const appOrigin = app.isPackaged ? 'file://' : 'http://localhost:5173'
148	
149	    if (url.startsWith(appOrigin)) {
150	      return { action: 'allow' }
151	    }
152	
153	    // Open external URLs in system browser by default
154	    shell.openExternal(url)
155	    return { action: 'deny' }
156	  })
157	
158	  if (!app.isPackaged) {
159	    await win.loadURL('http://localhost:5173') // Vite dev server (renderer)
160	    win.webContents.openDevTools({ mode: 'detach' })
161	  } else {
162	    await win.loadFile(path.join(process.resourcesPath, 'web/index.html'))
163	  }
164	
165	  // Check for updates after app loads (only in production)
166	  if (app.isPackaged) {
167	    setTimeout(() => {
168	      lastUpdateCheck = Date.now()
169	      autoUpdater.checkForUpdates()
170	    }, 3000) // Wait 3 seconds after launch
171	  }
172	
173	  // Check for updates when window becomes visible/focused (throttled to 15 minutes)
174	  win.on('focus', () => {
175	    if (app.isPackaged) {
176	      const now = Date.now()
177	      const fifteenMinutes = 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes in milliseconds
178	
179	      // Only check if 15 minutes have passed since last check
180	      if (now - lastUpdateCheck >= fifteenMinutes) {
181	        lastUpdateCheck = now
182	        autoUpdater.checkForUpdates()
183	      }
184	    }
185	  })
186	}
187	
188	// Example typed IPC
189	ipcMain.handle('app:getVersion', () => app.getVersion())
190	
191	// Open URL in default browser
192	ipcMain.handle('shell:openExternal', async (_event, url: string) => {
193	  await shell.openExternal(url)
194	})
195	
196	// Open URL in modal window (for Stripe, billing, etc.)
197	ipcMain.handle('shell:openInWindow', async (_event, url: string, options?: { title?: string }) => {
198	  const modalWindow = new BrowserWindow({
199	    width: 1000,
200	    height: 800,
201	    title: options?.title || 'Payment',
202	    parent: win || undefined,
203	    modal: false,
204	    webPreferences: {
205	      nodeIntegration: false,
206	      contextIsolation: true,
207	      sandbox: true,
208	    },
209	  })
210	
211	  await modalWindow.loadURL(url)
212	
213	  // Focus parent window when modal closes and notify renderer
214	  modalWindow.on('closed', () => {
215	    if (win && !win.isDestroyed()) {
216	      win.focus()
217	      win.webContents.send('window:closed')
218	    }
219	  })
220	})
221	
222	// Update IPC handlers
223	ipcMain.handle('update:check', async () => {
224	  if (!app.isPackaged) {
225	    return { available: false, message: 'Updates only available in production' }
226	  }
227	  return await autoUpdater.checkForUpdates()
228	})
229	
230	ipcMain.handle('update:download', async () => {
231	  return await autoUpdater.downloadUpdate()
232	})
233	
234	ipcMain.handle('update:install', () => {
235	  setImmediate(() => autoUpdater.quitAndInstall())
236	})
237	
238	// Auth IPC handlers
239	ipcMain.handle('auth:startGoogleSignIn', async () => {
240	  try {
241	    // Generate client nonce
242	    const clientNonce = crypto.randomUUID()
243	
244	    // Call /authStart to get OAuth URL and session ID
245	    const response = await fetch(`${CF_BASE}/authStart`, {
246	      method: 'POST',
247	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
248	      body: JSON.stringify({ clientNonce }),
249	    })
250	
251	    if (!response.ok) {
252	      throw new Error(`Failed to start auth: ${response.statusText}`)
253	    }
254	
255	    const { authorizeUrl, sid } = await response.json() as { authorizeUrl: string; sid: string }
256	
257	    // Store session locally
258	    const sessionPromise = new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) => {
259	      authSessions.set(sid, {
260	        sid,
261	        clientNonce,
262	        createdAt: Date.now(),
263	        resolver: resolve,
264	      })
265	
266	      // Timeout after 10 minutes
267	      setTimeout(() => {
268	        const session = authSessions.get(sid)
269	        if (session) {
270	          authSessions.delete(sid)
271	          reject(new Error('Authentication timeout'))
272	        }
273	      }, 10 * 60 * 1000)
274	    })
275	
276	    // Open system browser
277	    await shell.openExternal(authorizeUrl)
278	
279	    // Wait for callback
280	    const returnedSid = await sessionPromise
281	
282	    // Exchange sid for custom token
283	    const exchangeResponse = await fetch(`${CF_BASE}/authExchange`, {
284	      method: 'POST',
285	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
286	      body: JSON.stringify({ sid: returnedSid, clientNonce }),
287	    })
288	
289	    if (!exchangeResponse.ok) {
290	      const error = await exchangeResponse.json() as { error?: string }
291	      throw new Error(error.error || 'Failed to exchange token')
292	    }
293	
294	    const { customToken } = await exchangeResponse.json() as { customToken: string }
295	    return customToken
296	  } catch (error) {
297	    console.error('Auth flow error:', error)
298	    throw error
299	  }
300	})
301	
302	app.whenReady().then(createWindow)
303	app.on('window-all-closed', () => {
304	  if (process.platform !== 'darwin') app.quit()
305	})
306	app.on('activate', () => {
307	  if (BrowserWindow.getAllWindows().length === 0) createWindow()
308	})

I've completed the web search for your query about Firebase Authentication OIDC provider setup for LinkedIn with OpenID Connect. Here's what I found:

## Key Findings:

### Firebase OIDC Setup Process
To enable OpenID Connect in Firebase, go to the Sign-in providers page of the Firebase console, click Add new provider, and then click OpenID Connect. You need to select whether you will be using the authorization code flow or the implicit grant flow, and you should always use the code flow if your provider supports it.

### Required Configuration Details
You need to fill out details like "Name", "Client ID", "Issuer (URL)" and "Client secret". The Issuer value must be a URL that, when appended with /.well-known/openid-configuration, is the location of the provider's OIDC discovery document.

### Important LinkedIn Compatibility Note
There's a known compatibility issue: Firebase Auth OpenID Connect requires openid-configuration to be hosted at %issuer%/.well-known/openid-configuration, however, LinkedIn OIDC hosts the config file at %issuer%/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration which seems to make authentication with LinkedIn incompatible with Firebase Auth and GCP Identity Platform.

### Alternative Approach
An alternative solution involves authenticating users with LinkedIn Sign-In via Firebase Custom Auth tokens, using LinkedIn's OpenID Connect (OIDC) flow and Firebase Functions.

The search results don't contain 2025-specific console updates, but the core Firebase OIDC setup process remains consistent across versions.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

## Key Findings:

### Firebase OIDC Setup Process
To enable OpenID Connect in Firebase, go to the Sign-in providers page of the Firebase console, click Add new provider, and then click OpenID Connect. You need to select whether you will be using the authorization code flow or the implicit grant flow, and you should always use the code flow if your provider supports it.

### Required Configuration Details
You need to fill out details like "Name", "Client ID", "Issuer (URL)" and "Client secret". The Issuer value must be a URL that, when appended with /.well-known/openid-configuration, is the location of the provider's OIDC discovery document.

### Important LinkedIn Compatibility Note
There's a known compatibility issue: Firebase Auth OpenID Connect requires openid-configuration to be hosted at %issuer%/.well-known/openid-configuration, however, LinkedIn OIDC hosts the config file at %issuer%/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration which seems to make authentication with LinkedIn incompatible with Firebase Auth and GCP Identity Platform.

### Alternative Approach
An alternative solution involves authenticating users with LinkedIn Sign-In via Firebase Custom Auth tokens, using LinkedIn's OpenID Connect (OIDC) flow and Firebase Functions.

The search results don't contain 2025-specific console updates, but the core Firebase OIDC setup process remains consistent across versions.

input.prompt: What is the approach for LinkedIn OIDC + Firebase custom auth? What are the Cloud Function endpoints needed? What LinkedIn API URLs are used? What scopes are needed?

lastPrompt: We are going to add LinkedIn as an auth provider

content.content:
# LinkedIn OIDC + Firebase Custom Auth Approach

## Overall Strategy
The approach uses LinkedIn's OIDC provider to obtain an ID token, which is then verified and exchanged for a Firebase custom token, enabling seamless authentication in Next.js applications.

## Cloud Function Endpoints

**Single endpoint:** `/api/linkedin`
- Handles the OAuth callback
- Processes the authorization code
- Exchanges it for tokens
- Returns a redirect to `/linkedin-redirect`

## LinkedIn API URLs

- **Authorization:** `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization`
- **Token/OIDC discovery:** `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth`
- **JWKS endpoint:** `"https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks"`

## Required Scopes

The implementation requests: `"email openid profile"`

These scopes enable access to user identity information, email address, and profile details needed for account creation and authentication purposes.

toolUseResult.result:
# LinkedIn OIDC + Firebase Custom Auth Approach

## Overall Strategy
The approach uses LinkedIn's OIDC provider to obtain an ID token, which is then verified and exchanged for a Firebase custom token, enabling seamless authentication in Next.js applications.

## Cloud Function Endpoints

**Single endpoint:** `/api/linkedin`
- Handles the OAuth callback
- Processes the authorization code
- Exchanges it for tokens
- Returns a redirect to `/linkedin-redirect`

## LinkedIn API URLs

- **Authorization:** `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization`
- **Token/OIDC discovery:** `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth`
- **JWKS endpoint:** `"https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks"`

## Required Scopes

The implementation requests: `"email openid profile"`

These scopes enable access to user identity information, email address, and profile details needed for account creation and authentication purposes.

input.content:
# Add LinkedIn as Auth Provider

## Context

Firebase does **not** natively support LinkedIn as an identity provider, and LinkedIn's OIDC discovery URL is non-standard (`/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration` instead of `/.well-known/openid-configuration`), making Firebase's built-in OIDC provider incompatible. The solution is a **custom OAuth flow via Cloud Functions**, similar to the existing Google auth flow used for Electron.

## Architecture

LinkedIn uses OAuth 2.0 with OpenID Connect. The flow:
1. User clicks "Sign in with LinkedIn" → redirected to LinkedIn authorization URL
2. LinkedIn redirects back to our Cloud Function callback with an auth code
3. Cloud Function exchanges code for tokens, verifies the ID token, creates/finds Firebase user, generates custom token
4. Web: redirect back to app with session ID; Electron: deep link callback (same as Google flow)

### LinkedIn OAuth Details
- **Authorization URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization`
- **Token URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken`
- **JWKS URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks`
- **Scopes**: `openid email profile`
- **Response type**: `code`

## Implementation Steps

### 1. LinkedIn Developer App Setup (Manual)
- [ ] Go to [LinkedIn Developer Portal](https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps) and create an app (or use existing)
- [ ] Under "Auth" tab, add the callback URL: `https://us-central1-divergent-todos.cloudfunctions.net/linkedinAuthCallback`
- [ ] Under "Products", request access to "Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect"
- [ ] Note the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**
- [ ] Add secrets to Firebase: 
  ```bash
  cd apps/web
  firebase functions:secrets:set LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID
  firebase functions:secrets:set LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET
  ```

### 2. Cloud Functions — `functions/src/index.ts`

- [ ] Add LinkedIn secrets at the top:
  ```typescript
  const linkedinClientId = defineSecret("LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID");
  const linkedinClientSecret = defineSecret("LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET");
  ```

- [ ] Add `linkedinAuthStart` endpoint (similar to `authStart`):
  - Accepts `{ clientNonce }` 
  - Generates `sid` and `state`, stores session in `authSessions` collection
  - Builds LinkedIn authorization URL:
    ```
    https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization?
      response_type=code&
      client_id=...&
      redirect_uri=...linkedinAuthCallback&
      state=...&
      scope=openid%20email%20profile
    ```
  - Returns `{ authorizeUrl, sid }`
  - Secrets: `[linkedinClientId]`

- [ ] Add `linkedinAuthCallback` endpoint (similar to `authCallback`):
  - Receives `code` and `state` from LinkedIn redirect
  - Validates session by `state`
  - Exchanges code for tokens via POST to `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken`
  - Verifies the ID token using LinkedIn's JWKS (`https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks`) — use `jose` or `jsonwebtoken` + `jwks-rsa` library
  - Extracts `email`, `name`, `picture` from the ID token payload
  - Creates or finds Firebase user (`auth.getUserByEmail` / `auth.createUser`)
  - Creates custom token via `auth.createCustomToken`
  - Stores token in session
  - **Web flow**: Redirects to app URL with `sid` as query param (e.g., `https://divergent-todos.com/auth/linkedin/callback?sid=...`)
  - **Electron flow**: Redirects to deep link `divergent-todos://auth/callback?sid=...`
  - Secrets: `[linkedinClientId, linkedinClientSecret]`

  **Note**: The callback needs to know whether the flow was initiated from web or Electron. Add a `platform` field to the session (set during `linkedinAuthStart`).

- [ ] The existing `authExchange` endpoint can be reused as-is (it's provider-agnostic — just exchanges `sid` + `clientNonce` for `customToken`)

### 3. Install JWT verification library in functions

- [ ] Add `jose` package for JWKS-based JWT verification:
  ```bash
  cd functions
  pnpm add jose
  ```

### 4. Web App — New `useSignInWithLinkedIn` hook

- [ ] Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useSignInWithLinkedIn.ts`:
  - For **web**: Call `linkedinAuthStart` Cloud Function, get `authorizeUrl`, redirect the browser to it via `window.location.href`
  - The LinkedIn callback redirects back to the app at `/auth/linkedin/callback?sid=...`
  - A route handler or effect picks up the `sid`, calls `authExchange` to get the custom token, then calls `signInWithCustomToken`
  - For **Electron**: Similar to Google — call `linkedinAuthStart`, open system browser, wait for deep link callback with `sid`, then exchange

- [ ] Create `apps/web/src/LinkedInAuthCallback.tsx` (or handle in existing routing):
  - Reads `sid` from URL params
  - Needs `clientNonce` — store it in `sessionStorage` before redirect
  - Calls `authExchange` with `{ sid, clientNonce }` 
  - Calls `signInWithCustomToken(auth, customToken)`
  - Redirects to main app

### 5. Web App — Update `useSignIn.ts` to support provider selection

- [ ] Refactor `useSignIn` to accept a `provider` parameter (`"google" | "linkedin"`) OR keep separate hooks
- [ ] Recommended: Keep `useSignIn` for Google and create `useSignInWithLinkedIn` as a separate hook for clarity

### 6. Update `useLinkAnonymousAccount.ts`

- [ ] Add LinkedIn as an option for linking anonymous accounts (same pattern as Google linking)

### 7. Desktop App — Electron IPC for LinkedIn

- [ ] Add `auth:startLinkedInSignIn` IPC handler in `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts`:
  - Same pattern as `auth:startGoogleSignIn` but calls `linkedinAuthStart` instead of `authStart`
  - The deep link callback (`divergent-todos://auth/callback?sid=...`) already works — same handler

- [ ] Update preload script `apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts`:
  ```typescript
  startLinkedInSignIn: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('auth:startLinkedInSignIn'),
  ```

- [ ] Update IPC types `apps/desktop/src/types/ipc.d.ts`:
  ```typescript
  startLinkedInSignIn: () => Promise<string>
  ```

### 8. UI Updates

- [ ] Update `apps/web/src/AuthModal.tsx`:
  - Add "Sign in with LinkedIn" button with LinkedIn logo SVG
  - Wire to `useSignInWithLinkedIn` hook
  - Add LinkedIn option for anonymous account linking

- [ ] Update `apps/web/src/LandingPage.tsx`:
  - Add LinkedIn sign-in button alongside Google button

### 9. Analytics

- [ ] Update `trackSignIn` call to support `"linkedin"` provider in `apps/web/src/firebase/analytics.ts`

### 10. Routing (Web callback)

- [ ] Add a route for `/auth/linkedin/callback` in the app's routing to handle the OAuth redirect back from LinkedIn (web flow only — Electron uses deep links)

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `functions/src/index.ts` | Add `linkedinAuthStart`, `linkedinAuthCallback` endpoints + secrets |
| `functions/package.json` | Add `jose` dependency |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useSignInWithLinkedIn.ts` | **New** — LinkedIn sign-in hook |
| `apps/web/src/LinkedInAuthCallback.tsx` | **New** — OAuth callback handler page |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useLinkAnonymousAccount.ts` | Add LinkedIn linking support |
| `apps/web/src/AuthModal.tsx` | Add LinkedIn button |
| `apps/web/src/LandingPage.tsx` | Add LinkedIn button |
| `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts` | Add `auth:startLinkedInSignIn` IPC handler |
| `apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts` | Expose `startLinkedInSignIn` |
| `apps/desktop/src/types/ipc.d.ts` | Add type for `startLinkedInSignIn` |

## Deployment Order

1. Set LinkedIn secrets in Firebase
2. Deploy Cloud Functions (`linkedinAuthStart`, `linkedinAuthCallback`)
3. Deploy web app with new UI and callback route
4. Build and release desktop app with new IPC handler

# Add LinkedIn as Auth Provider

## Context

Firebase does **not** natively support LinkedIn as an identity provider, and LinkedIn's OIDC discovery URL is non-standard (`/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration` instead of `/.well-known/openid-configuration`), making Firebase's built-in OIDC provider incompatible. The solution is a **custom OAuth flow via Cloud Functions**, similar to the existing Google auth flow used for Electron.

## Architecture

LinkedIn uses OAuth 2.0 with OpenID Connect. The flow:
1. User clicks "Sign in with LinkedIn" → redirected to LinkedIn authorization URL
2. LinkedIn redirects back to our Cloud Function callback with an auth code
3. Cloud Function exchanges code for tokens, verifies the ID token, creates/finds Firebase user, generates custom token
4. Web: redirect back to app with session ID; Electron: deep link callback (same as Google flow)

### LinkedIn OAuth Details
- **Authorization URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization`
- **Token URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken`
- **JWKS URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks`
- **Scopes**: `openid email profile`
- **Response type**: `code`

## Implementation Steps

### 1. LinkedIn Developer App Setup (Manual)

- [ ] Add secrets to Firebase:

  ```bash
  firebase functions:secrets:set LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET
  ```

### 2. Cloud Functions — `functions/src/index.ts`

- [ ] Add LinkedIn secrets at the top:


  - Accepts `{ clientNonce }`

- [ ] Add `linkedinAuthCallback` endpoint (similar to `authCallback`):
  - Receives `code` and `state` from LinkedIn redirect
  - Validates session by `state`
  - Exchanges code for tokens via POST to `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken`
  - Verifies the ID token using LinkedIn's JWKS (`https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks`) — use `jose` library
  - Extracts `email`, `name`, `picture` from the ID token payload
  - Creates or finds Firebase user (`auth.getUserByEmail` / `auth.createUser`)
  - Creates custom token via `auth.createCustomToken`
  - Stores token in session
  - **Web flow**: Redirects to app URL with `sid` as query param (e.g., `https://divergent-todos.com/auth/linkedin/callback?sid=...`)
  - **Electron flow**: Redirects to deep link `divergent-todos://auth/callback?sid=...`

  **Note**: The callback needs to know whether the flow was initiated from web or Electron. Add a `platform` field to the session (set during `linkedinAuthStart`).

- [ ] The existing `authExchange` endpoint can be reused as-is (it's provider-agnostic — just exchanges `sid` + `clientNonce` for `customToken`)

### 3. Install JWT verification library in functions

- [ ] Add `jose` package for JWKS-based JWT verification:
  ```bash
  cd functions
  pnpm add jose
  ```

### 4. Web App — New `useSignInWithLinkedIn` hook

- [ ] Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useSignInWithLinkedIn.ts`:
  - For **web**: Call `linkedinAuthStart` Cloud Function, get `authorizeUrl`, redirect the browser to it via `window.location.href`. Store `clientNonce` in `sessionStorage` before redirect.
  - For **Electron**: Similar to Google — call `linkedinAuthStart` via IPC, open system browser, wait for deep link callback with `sid`, then exchange

- [ ] Create `apps/web/src/LinkedInAuthCallback.tsx` (or handle in existing routing):

### 5. Update `useLinkAnonymousAccount.ts`

edits.new_string:
# Add LinkedIn as Auth Provider

## Context

Firebase does **not** natively support LinkedIn as an identity provider, and LinkedIn's OIDC discovery URL is non-standard (`/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration` instead of `/.well-known/openid-configuration`), making Firebase's built-in OIDC provider incompatible. The solution is a **custom OAuth flow via Cloud Functions**, similar to the existing Google auth flow used for Electron.

## Architecture

LinkedIn uses OAuth 2.0 with OpenID Connect. The flow:
1. User clicks "Sign in with LinkedIn" → redirected to LinkedIn authorization URL
2. LinkedIn redirects back to our Cloud Function callback with an auth code
3. Cloud Function exchanges code for tokens, verifies the ID token, creates/finds Firebase user, generates custom token
4. Web: redirect back to app with session ID; Electron: deep link callback (same as Google flow)

### LinkedIn OAuth Details
- **Authorization URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization`
- **Token URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken`
- **JWKS URL**: `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks`
- **Scopes**: `openid email profile`
- **Response type**: `code`

## Implementation Steps

### 1. LinkedIn Developer App Setup (Manual)
- [ ] Go to [LinkedIn Developer Portal](https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps) and create an app (or use existing)
- [ ] Under "Auth" tab, add the callback URL: `https://us-central1-divergent-todos.cloudfunctions.net/linkedinAuthCallback`
- [ ] Under "Products", request access to "Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect"
- [ ] Note the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**
- [ ] Add secrets to Firebase:
  ```bash
  cd apps/web
  firebase functions:secrets:set LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID
  firebase functions:secrets:set LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET
  ```

### 2. Cloud Functions — `functions/src/index.ts`

- [ ] Add LinkedIn secrets at the top:
  ```typescript
  const linkedinClientId = defineSecret("LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID");
  const linkedinClientSecret = defineSecret("LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET");
  ```

- [ ] Add `linkedinAuthStart` endpoint (similar to `authStart`):
  - Accepts `{ clientNonce, platform }` (`platform` = `"web"` or `"electron"`)
  - Generates `sid` and `state`, stores session in `authSessions` collection (include `platform`)
  - Builds LinkedIn authorization URL:
    ```
    https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization?
      response_type=code&
      client_id=...&
      redirect_uri=...linkedinAuthCallback&
      state=...&
      scope=openid%20email%20profile
    ```
  - Returns `{ authorizeUrl, sid }`
  - Secrets: `[linkedinClientId]`

- [ ] Add `linkedinAuthCallback` endpoint (similar to `authCallback`):
  - Receives `code` and `state` from LinkedIn redirect
  - Validates session by `state`
  - Exchanges code for tokens via POST to `https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken`
  - Verifies the ID token using LinkedIn's JWKS (`https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/openid/jwks`) — use `jose` library
  - Extracts `email`, `name`, `picture` from the ID token payload
  - Creates or finds Firebase user (`auth.getUserByEmail` / `auth.createUser`)
  - Creates custom token via `auth.createCustomToken`
  - Stores token in session
  - **Web flow** (`platform === "web"`): Redirects to `https://divergent-todos.com/auth/linkedin/callback?sid=...`
  - **Electron flow** (`platform === "electron"`): Redirects to deep link `divergent-todos://auth/callback?sid=...`
  - Secrets: `[linkedinClientId, linkedinClientSecret]`

- [ ] The existing `authExchange` endpoint can be reused as-is (it's provider-agnostic)

### 3. Install JWT verification library in functions

- [ ] Add `jose` package for JWKS-based JWT verification:
  ```bash
  cd functions
  pnpm add jose
  ```

### 4. Web App — New `useSignInWithLinkedIn` hook

- [ ] Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useSignInWithLinkedIn.ts`:
  - For **web**: Call `linkedinAuthStart` endpoint (via fetch), store `clientNonce` + `sid` in `sessionStorage`, redirect browser to `authorizeUrl`
  - For **Electron**: Call via IPC `window.native.auth.startLinkedInSignIn()`, get back custom token, call `signInWithCustomToken`

- [ ] Create `apps/web/src/LinkedInAuthCallback.tsx`:
  - Reads `sid` from URL search params
  - Retrieves `clientNonce` from `sessionStorage`
  - Calls `authExchange` with `{ sid, clientNonce }`
  - Calls `signInWithCustomToken(auth, customToken)`
  - Initialize profile document (same as Google flow)
  - Redirects to main app

### 5. Update `useLinkAnonymousAccount.ts`

- [ ] Add LinkedIn as an option for linking anonymous accounts (accept `provider` parameter)

### 6. Desktop App — Electron IPC for LinkedIn

- [ ] Add `auth:startLinkedInSignIn` IPC handler in `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts`:
  - Same pattern as `auth:startGoogleSignIn` but calls `linkedinAuthStart` (with `platform: "electron"`)
  - Reuses the same deep link callback handler

- [ ] Update preload script `apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts`:
  ```typescript
  startLinkedInSignIn: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('auth:startLinkedInSignIn'),
  ```

- [ ] Update IPC types `apps/desktop/src/types/ipc.d.ts`:
  ```typescript
  startLinkedInSignIn: () => Promise<string>
  ```

### 7. UI Updates

- [ ] Update `apps/web/src/AuthModal.tsx`:
  - Add "Sign in with LinkedIn" button with LinkedIn logo SVG (blue `#0A66C2`)
  - Wire to `useSignInWithLinkedIn` hook
  - Add LinkedIn option for anonymous account linking

- [ ] Update `apps/web/src/LandingPage.tsx`:
  - Add LinkedIn sign-in button alongside Google button

### 8. Analytics

- [ ] Update `trackSignIn` to support `"linkedin"` provider value

### 9. Routing (Web callback)

- [ ] Add a route for `/auth/linkedin/callback` that renders `LinkedInAuthCallback` component

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `functions/src/index.ts` | Add `linkedinAuthStart`, `linkedinAuthCallback` + secrets |
| `functions/package.json` | Add `jose` dependency |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useSignInWithLinkedIn.ts` | **New** — LinkedIn sign-in hook |
| `apps/web/src/LinkedInAuthCallback.tsx` | **New** — OAuth callback handler page |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useLinkAnonymousAccount.ts` | Add LinkedIn linking support |
| `apps/web/src/AuthModal.tsx` | Add LinkedIn button |
| `apps/web/src/LandingPage.tsx` | Add LinkedIn button |
| `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts` | Add `auth:startLinkedInSignIn` IPC handler |
| `apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts` | Expose `startLinkedInSignIn` |
| `apps/desktop/src/types/ipc.d.ts` | Add type for `startLinkedInSignIn` |

## Deployment Order

1. Set LinkedIn secrets in Firebase
2. Deploy Cloud Functions (`linkedinAuthStart`, `linkedinAuthCallback`)
3. Deploy web app with new UI and callback route
4. Build and release desktop app with new IPC handler
